one_time_description: Correction of error: Marketable securities/portfolio investments understated by $1,000,000 due to not recognizing direct ownership in common shares with public price quotations; prior year restated.
economic_notes: Ontario First Nation (2008) Limited Partnership distributions received. Economic Development segment reported. Wasaya Fuel Delivery fees and Hydro One Maintenance revenue suggest community utility operations. Exchange Income Group dividends received.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not identified/assessed; (2) tangible capital asset register incomplete so unrecorded assets and amortization cannot be determined. Large correction of error restating 2022 figures: marketable securities understated by $1,000,000. Consolidated entities are Wapekeka Admin, Education, Health, and Ontario Works departments only. Ontario First Nation (2008) Limited Partnership appears to be accounted for as a revenue line (partnership distributions), not consolidated. Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 but audit report signed May 14, 2025 — significant delay of two years. Capital Projects - Housing segment shows zero actual expenses vs $665,261 budget. $28.5M of tangible capital assets are under construction and not being amortized. accounts_receivable includes amounts due from government ($542,412) plus trade and other receivables ($1,305,930). current_liabilities = bank indebtedness ($638,408) + accounts payable ($3,675,809). isc_funding per revenue line; total_government_transfers = ISC + CMHC + provincial ministries; osr = total revenue minus government transfers.
